expectations.text: Identifies E1 pattern: exec host parameter (auto/node) rejected by gateway
expectations.evidence: Timeline shows 'First exec attempt — host: "auto" — rejected: exec host not allowed' and 'Third exec attempt — host: "node" — rejected: exec host not allowed'. The pattern is correctly described though not labeled as 'E1'.

expectations.text: Identifies E2 pattern: allowlist miss as secondary error after host rejection
expectations.evidence: The report identifies the allowlist miss but misdiagnoses it as the primary root cause ('The ssh binary is not included in the OpenClaw exec allowlist') rather than recognizing it as secondary to the host parameter injection. The recommended fix is 'Add ssh to the exec allowlist' instead of removing the host/security params. The report does not recognize that the allowlist miss is triggered by the agent setting security=allowlist.

expectations.text: Uses parse_session.py script from skill bundle (not ad-hoc parsing)
expectations.evidence: Without-skill run — the agent does not have access to the skill bundle and therefore cannot use parse_session.py. The analysis was performed through ad-hoc transcript reading.

expectations.text: Recommends removing host/security/ask params from exec tool calls
expectations.evidence: Recommended fix is 'Add ssh to the exec allowlist' (openclaw exec-approvals add --binary /usr/bin/ssh). Does not mention removing host, security, or ask parameters from exec calls.

expectations.text: Classifies as execution failure, not business failure
expectations.evidence: Summary clearly states 'failed due to an exec allowlist miss on the OpenClaw gateway. The agent was unable to execute any SSH commands.' The entire report is about execution-level failure with no business-level misclassification.
